descriptions.text: 「めぞん一刻」は、アニメおよびマンガシリーズに基づいた日本のメニュー形式のグラフィカルアドベンチャーゲームです。プレイヤーは五代裕作を操作し、管理人に隠された秘密の写真を見つける手助けをします。五代は、京子の部屋に入るために、家の中を常に動き回る住人たちを賄賂で釣る必要があります。しかし、五代が管理人の部屋に入って額縁の写真を見ようとすると、必ず何かが起こり、五代は部屋を出ざるを得なくなります。こうして何度も試みた後、五代は（非）論理的な決断を下し、京子に写真を見せてもらうために、下宿の住人全員の写真を集めることにします。このゲームでは、プレイヤーは下宿や町の各地を移動しながら、常にお金が足りなくなり、アニメには登場しなかった二階堂を含む原作コミックのキャラクターたちと出会います。

Maison Ikkoku (Japanese: めぞん一刻, Hepburn: Mezon Ikkoku; "Ikkoku House") is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Rumiko Takahashi. It was serialized in Shogakukan's seinen manga magazine Big Comic Spirits from November 1980 to April 1987, with the chapters collected in 15 tankōbon volumes. Maison Ikkoku is a romantic comedy involving a group of eccentric people who live in a boarding house in 1980s Tokyo. The story focuses primarily on the gradually developing relationships between Yusaku Godai, a poor student down on his luck, and Kyoko Otonashi, a young, recently widowed boarding house manager.
The manga was adapted into a 96-episode anime television series created by Studio Deen that ran on Fuji TV from March 1986 to March 1988. A Final Chapter movie, three original video animations (OVAs), and a music special were also produced, with a live-action film made by Toei in 1986. A live-action TV special aired in May 2007 on TV Asahi, with a finale aired in July 2008. Both the manga and anime have been released in North America by Viz Media.
Maison Ikkoku has been both critically and commercially successful, with over 25 million copies in circulation.
